title: Velvety German Beef Stew
description: A malty, smoky German stew that soothes hangovers with hearty beef, beer, and a touch of jerk-inspired warmth.
instructions: Season the beef cubes with salt, pepper, smoked paprika, cumin, coriander, fennel seeds, and smoked salt, then toss to coat evenly.
Heat the olive oil in a large Dutch oven over medium-high heat and brown the beef in batches, removing them to a plate as they brown.
In the same pot, add the diced onion and minced garlic and sauté until the onion becomes translucent.
Pour in the dark lager and use a wooden spoon to scrape up all the browned bits from the bottom of the pot.
Return the beef to the pot along with the sliced carrots, celery, thyme, and a pinch of smoked paprika, then stir to combine.
Add the diced potatoes, beef broth, and Worcestershire sauce, then bring the mixture to a gentle simmer.
Cover the pot, reduce the heat to low, and let the stew cook for 90 minutes, stirring occasionally.
Halfway through the simmer, drizzle honey to enhance the malty sweetness and stir thoroughly.
After the total cooking time, taste and adjust seasoning with additional smoked paprika, salt, and pepper if needed.
Serve the velvety beef stew hot, optionally garnished with fresh thyme and a sprinkle of smoked salt.
